factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Trump announced ceasefire agreement with Iran; Strait of Hormuz reopening set for June 19, eliminating 4-month war premium and supply shock. Oil down >4% on deal (WTI/Brent heavy selling), reducing energy input costs for Dow industrials and transportation stocks.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: Crude down 3-4% as Hormuz reopening removes geopolitical premium and Iranian sanctions eased. Lower energy costs benefit CVX dividend, reduce inflation/input pressures for CAT, BA, transportation, and improve real yields backdrop.

factors.name: Equity Futures Rally on Deal News
factors.summary: ES and NQ up 0.7% and 1.0% respectively in Globex after Iran deal announcement (June 14 22:03 UTC). Dow futures likely tracking higher; positive open expected Monday June 16.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: Dollar marked lower in early Asia trade on peace news. While this can weigh short-term on USD-based equities translation, it reflects risk-on sentiment and lower safe-haven demand—net bullish for risk assets like equities.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Kevin Warsh Uncertainty
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh takes helm as new Fed Chair this week; MarketWatch notes economists don't know what to expect. FOMC meeting and press conference upcoming (likely Tue/Wed)—macro significance high but direction TBD pending guidance.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Long-End Yields
factors.summary: No major inflation print or Fed surprise yet in news window. Lower oil (disinflationary) may ease rate-hike fears, supporting dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JPM, GS), but Warsh presser could shift expectations sharply.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings Outlook
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings reported in news window. Earnings season typically in April/May; June is quiet. Forward guidance from industrials (CAT, BA, HON) will be key post-deal narrative (improved order outlook on stabilized global backdrop).

factors.name: China Growth / Trade Data
factors.summary: No recent PMI or trade releases in news. However, Iran deal + oil decline + potential Fed dovish lean could improve EM/China appetite; Caterpillar and Boeing could see demand boost if global manufacturing inflection seen.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment Reversal (Knicks Celebration / Civil Unrest Minor)
factors.summary: NYC Knicks championship triggered some celebratory disorder; 63 arrests, 1 teen shot. Economically immaterial but suggests retail risk appetite intact. Not a material macro driver for Dow.

factors.name: G7 Summit / Trump Tariff Narrative
factors.summary: Trump attending G7 in France this week after UFC event; agenda includes Ukraine, Iran, trade. Tariff threats remain latent but peace deal may delay escalation near-term. No new tariff news in window.

key_levels.support: 51,000 (4h MA zone), 50,700 (recent swing low before deal)

screening.narrative: The US-Iran peace deal announced late Sunday (June 14) was the dominant catalyst: oil slumped 3-4% on Hormuz reopening expectations, equity futures rallied 0.7-1%, and safe-haven demand (USD, duration) weakened. Dow futures up in sympathy, with industrial and energy names (CAT, CVX, BA, GS, JPM) set to benefit from lower input costs, reduced geopolitical premium, and improved global trade backdrop. However, the move is already underway in US30 (+0.30% 24h) and equity futures are bid, placing this in mid-Phase 2; real catalysts ahead include Warsh's first FOMC presser (mid-week) and any new headlines on Iran deal signing (set June 19). Risk: escalation from Israel/Iran (Netanyahu not pleased with Trump's pushback) or Warsh hawkishness could quickly reverse sentiment.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh FOMC Meeting & Press Conference (Tue/Wed June 17-18) — critical macro reset
US-Iran Deal Formal Signing (Friday June 19) — execution risk / renegotiation headlines
Potential Israel-Iran escalation (ongoing medium-term risk; Iran 'warned' strikes could resume)
Fed Fund Futures repricing into Warsh presser
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (if released early week) — Dow industrial health check
Dow component earnings guidance / conference calls (forward guidance on tariffs, global demand)
Oil price stability (watch for Iran oil re-entry timing and geopolitical spikes)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already underway (+0.30% 24h); if Warsh signals hawkish stance or tightens guidance, Dow could reverse sharply lower, especially if long-duration bonds (high-dividend Dow names like JNJ, UNH, MCD) reprice. Escalation from Israel-Iran (Netanyahu reportedly unhappy) or new tariff threats from Trump at G7 could also undercut rally. Wait for retest of support after initial enthusiasm fades.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway in Phase 2; wait for pullback to 51,000–51,100 support (4h MA) or intraday retest of 51,200 after open before adding longs. Avoid chasing extended moves into FOMC presser (mid-week volatility likely). Short-term longs favor on dips with stop below 50,800.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Enter on minor dip or at market open; peace deal premium not yet fully priced in US regular session, futures up 0.7%.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz and halt hostilities, reducing major geopolitical risk premium. Deal signing set for Friday June 19 in Switzerland. Trump confirmed Hormuz opening Friday upon signing.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil slumped over 4% on Hormuz reopening news and Iranian supply returning to market within weeks. Lower energy input costs are bullish for Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON, BA) and reduce inflation pressures. CVX drag offset by broader margin benefit.

factors.name: Equity Futures Response
factors.summary: ES futures up 0.7%, NQ up 1.0% in Sunday Globex session on peace deal announcement. Risk-on sentiment clearly dominant; Dow futures participating in relief rally as geopolitical tail risk removed.

factors.name: Supply Chain Normalization
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening (20% of global oil passes through) ends months-long supply chain disruption. LNG tanker already heading toward Hormuz. Bullish for Dow multinationals with Asia/Europe exposure and trade-sensitive industrials.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD marked lower in early Asia trading on peace news. Weaker dollar is positive for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure, improves translation effects for multinationals (MSFT, AAPL, CAT, BA).

factors.name: European Sanctions Relief
factors.summary: UK, France, Germany, Italy ready to lift relevant Iran sanctions per joint statement. Broader de-escalation and normalization of trade flows supports global growth outlook and Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Fed Meeting This Week
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting as chair this week creates uncertainty. WSJ notes 'economists honestly don't know what to expect.' Oil drop is disinflationary, potentially dovish signal, but Warsh policy stance unclear. Caps conviction until after FOMC.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk
factors.summary: Israel struck Beirut despite ceasefire talks; Iranian state TV threatened 'heavy bombing' of Israel tonight. Trump publicly rebuked Netanyahu as 'very difficult,' threatened to resume Iran strikes if nuclear accord fails. Deal fragility remains; 30-day Hormuz deadline and managed (not unconditional) reopening per MOU details.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: Dow at 51,202, up only +0.30% in 24h, -0.07% in 4h. Move has NOT extended yet; peace news broke Sunday evening US time, markets were closed. This is fresh catalyst entering Monday session.

factors.name: Dow Sector Composition
factors.summary: Dow's industrial-heavy composition (CAT, BA, HON, MMM, GE) benefits directly from lower oil/input costs and supply chain normalization. Financials (JPM, GS, V) benefit from risk-on and lower recession risk. CVX is only 1 of 30 names.

confirmation.narrative: US-Iran peace deal announced Sunday evening represents major positive catalyst for Dow, removing geopolitical premium that has weighed on markets for months. Strait of Hormuz reopening (set for Friday) collapses oil prices 4%+, directly benefiting Dow's industrial base through lower input costs and restoring critical supply chain link. Equity futures gapped higher 0.7% in thin Sunday Globex trade, signaling strong Monday open likely. However, deal fragility remains—Israel-Lebanon tensions flaring, Trump threatening to resume strikes if nuclear talks fail within 60 days, and managed (not unconditional) Hormuz reopening creates execution risk. Fed meeting this week with new Chair Warsh adds uncertainty, though oil collapse is disinflationary and potentially dovish.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting this week (Kevin Warsh's first as Fed Chair, policy stance unknown)
Iran peace deal signing Friday June 19 in Switzerland
Strait of Hormuz actual reopening Friday per Trump statement
Potential Iranian retaliation against Israel (state TV threatened 'heavy bombing tonight')
G7 summit this week in France (Trump attending, Iran deal likely focus)
confirmation.risk_warning: Deal collapses if Israel-Iran hostilities resume (Iranian state TV threatened strikes on Israel tonight), Trump resumes strikes, or Hormuz reopening delayed beyond Friday. Hawkish surprise from Warsh at FOMC would override oil drop benefits. European demand for 'unconditional' Hormuz access conflicts with MOU terms of 'managed' reopening—potential friction point.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Risk-Off)
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MoU to reopen Strait of Hormuz, ending ~4 months of conflict. Global risk sentiment sharply improved; Asian equities surged (SoftBank +12%, Taiwan +2.36%), while safe-haven flows reversed (USD weakened, DXY ~99.50).

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI tumbled to 2-month low near $79.40 on Hormuz reopening and supply normalization expectations. Lower energy costs benefit Dow's cyclical/industrial names and reduce input cost inflation pressures.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: DXY fell from elevated levels as safe-haven demand faded post-deal. Weaker USD lifts multinational earnings translation for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (particularly CAT, BA, GE, JPM).

factors.name: Rate-Cut Expectations Lift (Bonds Rally)
factors.summary: Treasury yields advanced across curve as Fed rate-hike odds trimmed post-Iran deal. Lower long-end yields support dividend-payers and rate-sensitive Dow heavyweights (JPM, GS, utilities exposure).

factors.name: Trade Policy Uncertainty (Trump Tariff Threats)
factors.summary: Trump threatened France with 100% wine tariffs over tech 'sales tax'; G7 critical minerals pricing plan faces skepticism. Tariff escalation could pressure margin-sensitive industrials (CAT, BA, MMM, HON) and cross-border supply chains.

factors.name: Execution Risk on Deal (MoU, Not Signed)
factors.summary: Deal scheduled to sign Friday (2026-06-20); Netanyahu says Israel not bound by Lebanon ceasefire clause; Hormuz reopening gradual, not immediate, with unspecified verification. Market euphoria carries execution/geopolitical tail risk.

factors.name: Dow Price Action (Near-Flat, Move Not Fully Priced)
factors.summary: US30 +0.30% 24h, -0.07% 4h. Asian/EU equities surged on deal news; US futures jumped overnight but Dow itself has not yet fully participated in rally. Room for catch-up in US cash session.

factors.name: Economic Data Thin (No Major Releases in Window)
factors.summary: No CPI, ISM, NFP, or FOMC in immediate 24h news window. German wholesale prices +5.9% YoY (inflation still sticky), Swedish unemployment 9.4% (mixed). Lack of US hard data reduces conviction.

factors.name: SpaceX Debut Sentiment (Risk-On Momentum)
factors.summary: SpaceX surged past $2T market cap on Nasdaq debut; Gina Rinehart made 'significant' SpaceX investment. Tech/growth euphoria from Friday carry over, benefiting Dow growth and financials (JPM, MSFT on Dow).

factors.name: ECB Cautious Optimism (Lagarde on Inflation)
factors.summary: ECB welcomes Iran deal's inflation relief but warns of second-round effects in services. European sticky inflation narrative intact; benefits US Dow if Fed can stay dovish longer.

screening.narrative: The US-Iran peace deal triggered a sharp global risk-on shift overnight: geopolitical premium collapsed, oil crashed ~6%, safe-haven flows unwound, and Asian/European equities rallied 2–12%. The Dow has not yet fully caught up to this euphoria in US cash trading, offering near-term participation potential for cyclical and multinational names (CAT, BA, JPM, GE) that benefit from lower oil costs and stronger foreign earnings translation. However, execution risk on the MoU (signing Friday, Israel's non-compliance on Lebanon, gradual Hormuz clearing) and Trump's renewed tariff threats (France 100% wine tariff, G7 minerals skepticism) create a 'sell-the-news' tail. Current price action (flat-to-+0.3%) suggests Phase 2 momentum building but not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran Peace Deal Signing (Friday, 2026-06-20) — execution risk and any last-minute geopolitical flare-up
BOJ Rate Hike to 1% (Tuesday, 2026-06-17) — yen weakness could extend risk-on rally globally
G7 Summit (Évian-les-Bains, this week) — Trump tariff and critical minerals negotiations in real-time
ISM Manufacturing PMI (late week) — first hard US economic data post-deal; sticky inflation vs. cooling cycle
US Treasury Auction Cycle (ongoing) — yield sensitivity for Dow dividend payers and rate-exposed financials
China Municipal Property Stimulus Rolls (ongoing) — 470+ measures announced; impacts CAT, BA, materials demand
screening.risk_warning: If Israel escalates in Lebanon or Iran signals non-compliance before Friday signing, geopolitical premium snaps back, oil rallies, and Dow's euphoria reverses. Tariff escalation (Trump-France, G7 breakdown) could overwhelm energy-cost relief and hit industrials hard. ECB inflation concerns + sticky service-sector pricing could force Fed to hold rates higher for longer, pressuring valuations. Gap-fill risk if overnight rally doesn't hold into US cash open.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (2–4h old); wait for US cash open (09:30 ET) to confirm Dow participation in overnight rally. If US30 holds above 51100 at open, add on dips to 51000–51050. If momentum stalls or tariff headlines resurface, await retest of 51000 before re-entering.
